digest.top_line: SF-SABR pooled (N=1687): LC 90-93% at 2yr, G3+ AEs 2.9%; single-fraction technique validated across primary NSCLC and pulmonary oligomets.
digest.tldr: Thoracic and GU dominated today. SF-SABR confirmed at scale with durable LC and low toxicity; AUA 2026 review favors PSMA PET over ePLND for intermediate-risk staging. Real-world MIBC TMT (N=369) supports bladder preservation as RC alternative.

sites.intro: Largest single-fraction SABR pooled dataset to date; consistent LC and low-toxicity profile across primary NSCLC and pulmonary oligomets.

studies.name: Single-fraction SABR for primary NSCLC and pulmonary oligometastases (pooled analysis)
studies.tldr: SF-SABR (N=1687): LC 90-93% at 2yr, mOS 40mo (primary NSCLC) and 51mo (oligo), G3+ AEs 2.9%.
details: 🔍 3-institution pooled analysis (Peter Mac / Cleveland Clinic / Roswell Park): 1200 primary NSCLC, 487 pulmonary oligomets
⚠️ AE data available for primary NSCLC only (n=789); no Roswell Park AE data included
📊 G2+ AEs: 124/789 (15.7%); G3+: 23/789 (2.9%); chest wall pain most common at 14%
⚠️ Single-arm pooled retrospective; no randomised comparator vs. multi-fraction SABR
🔗 LC 90-93% at 2yr consistent with multi-fraction SBRT institutional series for early-stage NSCLC; oligo mOS >4yr aligns with prior oligomet SABR data

figures.caption: LC 90-93% at 2yr; NSCLC mPFS 30mo, mOS 3.5yr; oligo mPFS 11mo, OS >4yr; G3+ AEs 2-3%
AEs, primary NSCLC (n=789): G3+ 23/789 (2.9%), G2+ 124/789 (15.7%); chest wall pain 114 (14%), pneumonitis 52 (7%)

verdict.rationale: Large single-arm pooled analysis; no randomised comparator vs. multi-fraction SABR. Cross-institutional consistency encouraging but insufficient for confirmatory read.

open_questions: Randomised comparison vs. multi-fraction SABR needed to confirm equivalence
AE profile in oligometastases cohort not yet reported

sites.intro: PSMA PET displacing ePLND in intermediate-risk staging; lymphedema morbidity data now quantified on the decision tree alongside NPV.

studies.name: ePLND vs PSMA PET staging for prostate cancer (AUA 2026 review)
studies.tldr: AUA 2026 review: PSMA PET NPV ~96%, no consistent RCT BCR benefit from ePLND; intermediate-risk pts may safely omit PLND if PET-negative.
details: 📊 PSMA PET/CT NPV ~96% for LNI at primary staging (tweet-reported; primary source not cited in source tweets)
📊 47.7% of LN metastases fall outside the standard ePLND template (Yaxley BJUI 2019; N=1253 consecutive staging PET/CTs)
🔍 AUA 2026 state-of-the-art review synthesizing observational data, systematic reviews, and prior RCTs; no new primary trial
⚠️ Absent Level 1 evidence for significant oncological benefit from ePLND; RCTs have not shown consistent BCR improvements

details.text: ⚠️ ePLND morbidity: lymphedema by treatment context
subdetails: 0-14% lower limb lymphedema after RP + PLND
0-9% after pelvic lymph node RT alone
19-29% after PLND + salvage pelvic node RT (+ 2-22% genital lymphedema)
6-10x increased DVT/PE risk with PLND (Tyritzis J Urol 2015; N=3544)

details: 🔍 Intermediate-risk: PLND may safely be omitted if PSMA PET negative for LNI; missed LN may also fall outside ePLND template
🔍 GG3: nomograms add value alongside PSMA PET for individualized PLND decision
🔍 High-risk: individualized; adjuvant/salvage pelvic RT discussed as alternative pathway, especially without prior ePLND
🔗 Roberts PCAN 2024: PSMA PET-guided post-op pelvic RT plan as ePLND alternative, limiting lymphedema morbidity

figures.caption: Lymphedema prevalence after PCa treatment: 0-14% lower limb (RP+PLND); 19-29% after PLND+salvage pelvic RT; 6-10x DVT/PE risk with PLND (Tyritzis J Urol 2015; N=3544)
Yaxley BJUI 2019 (N=1253 primary staging Ga-PSMA PET/CTs): 47.7% of LN metastases outside ePLND template boundaries

verdict.rationale: AUA 2026 expert review challenges routine ePLND: PSMA PET NPV ~96%, 47.7% mets outside template, no Level 1 oncological benefit. Not a new RCT.
verdict.audience: Localized intermediate-high-risk PCa, primary staging, PSMA PET-eligible, planne

open_questions: Does PET-guided ePLND omission compromise oncological outcomes in high-risk pts?
Optimal PSMA PET threshold for safe PLND omission across risk groups?
Does post-op pelvic RT replace ePLND without sacrificing cancer control?

sites.intro: Spanish real-world cohort reinforces 5-FU CRT as preferred TMT regimen; image-guidance frequency as CLR predictor warrants prospective validation.

studies.name: Bladder-preserving TMT for muscle-invasive bladder cancer (ESTRO 2026 multicenter analysis)
studies.tldr: CLR 63.7% in N=369 MIBC TMT cohort; 5-FU CRT and image guidance independently predict CLR on MVA.
details: 📊 CLR 63.7%; overall disease progression 28.8%
📊 Salvage cystectomy 9.7%
📐 5-FU CRT → higher CLR: OR 4.9 (95% CI 1.1-22.1), p=0.038
📐 Weekly portal imaging → lower CLR: OR 0.35 (95% CI 0.20-0.60), p<0.001
🔗 CLR rate consistent with published European TMT series; real-world data supports bladder preservation as RC alternative
⚠️ Retrospective design; no RC comparator arm; TMT eligibility selection bias uncontrolled
⚠️ OS/CSS numbers not reported in source; comparable survival claim unverifiable from abstract

verdict.rationale: Retrospective multicenter cohort; no randomised RC comparator. TMT already guideline-endorsed; adds real-world European multicenter data.
verdict.audience: cT2-T4aN0M0 MIBC, bladder-preservation intent, median age 76

open_questions: Does daily IGRT vs weekly portal films improve CLR in prospective comparison?
OS/CSS vs contemporary radical cystectomy in matched MIBC pts?
Optimal concurrent chemotherapy (5-FU vs cisplatin) when image guidance is optimized?

Purpose
Trimodality therapy (TMT), combining maximal transurethral resection of the bladder tumor followed by concurrent chemoradiotherapy, has emerged as a bladder-preserving alternative to radical
cystectomy (RC) for muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC). Despite growing evidence, real-world multicenter data from European populations remain limited.

Conclusions
• For intermediate risk disease PLND may safely be omitted if PSMA PET is negative for LNI,
missed LN are small and may be missed in ePLND as well
• In GG3 disease nomograms can be of additional use
•
For high-risk disease, the decision is individual and should be discussed with the patient at
length including the possibility that an adjuvant/salvage pelvic RT may increase side effects
If PLND is to be performed, then it should be in extended fashion

Clinical
Is extended pelvic lymph node dissection REALLY required for
staging of prostate cancer in the PSMA-PET era?
Matthew J. Roberts 123, John W. Yaxley 134, Johan Stranne, Inge M. van Oort and Derya Tilki C89.10
Strengths
•Confirm metastases not detected by imaging (false-negatives)
• Accurate histopathological staging (false-positives)
• Total tumour burden reduction via excision (if nodal metastases
present)
Opportunities
•Incorporation with PSMA-PET imaging for targeted (Radioguided/
Sentinel) LND
• Improved patient selection considering prevalence of metastatic
disease (higher nomogram cut-off)
Weaknesses
• Limitation of anatomical template (missed metastases outside
template)
• Limitation of histopathology (may not detect all metastatic cells)
•Associated morbidity/complications, impact on health and quality of
life
• Increased intra-operative time
Threats
• Absent Level 1 evidence for significant oncological benefit
• Increasing demand on health services to limit low-value care/
interventions
• Surgeon proficiency/training
• Earlier use of androgen receptor signalling inhibitors and other
medications (pN status less relevant)
Alternative care pathways instead of routine eLND
PSMA-PET for staging may prompt a plan for post-operative pelvic lymph node radiotherapy,
limiting morbidity such as lymphedema, especially in the absence of a prior eND.
PCAN, 2024
